response.justification: Strong systems engineer with current Staff-level title at an AI-native shop (re:cinq) building MCP code generators and agentic tooling, which directly matches the role's mindset bar. Distributed/high-throughput credentials are real but dated (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby ad-tech, DLang-era). The biggest gap is Python: it is listed as a must-have, yet does not appear in the candidate's skills or recent stack — TypeScript/Node is the dominant language. Worth a screen to probe Python depth and current distributed-systems hands-on, but not an automatic yes.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic tooling experience at current Staff role
Proven high-throughput distributed-systems background (microservices handling millions of req/s at Sociomantic)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js depth plus craftsmanship habits (TDD, pairing, trunk-based CI/CD)
concerns: Python — a stated must-have — is absent from skills list and recent work history
Most recent distributed-systems-at-scale work was 5+ years ago; recent roles skew product/full-stack
Title is Staff but resume reads more as senior IC; limited explicit evidence of cross-functional technical strategy and stakeholder leadership

key_factors.factor: Current AI-agent / MCP / agentic tooling experience
